plans.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

columns.column_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
columns.extraction_plan: The class of agents in Treatment Arm 1 (Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel) is reported in the abstract and introduction. Darolutamide is an androgen receptor pathway inhibitor, ADT is androgen-deprivation therapy (a type of hormonal therapy), and docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent.
columns.column_name_raw: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

columns.extraction_plan: This column refers to the overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence in randomized controlled trials. This type of assessment (e.g., risk of bias assessment) is typically performed by external reviewers in systematic reviews or meta-analyses, not usually reported within a primary clinical trial publication itself. The ARASENS trial report does not contain an overall risk judgment of its own individual criteria of evidence.

The add-on treatment is Darolutamide. This is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 1: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." This indicates that darolutamide (or placebo) is added to the standard backbone of ADT and docetaxel. The "Trial Design" section on page 2 further confirms this: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel."

The control arm is explicitly stated in the abstract and methods section. It can also be inferred from the labels in figures and tables. On page 1, in the "METHODS" section of the abstract, it states: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." On page 2, in the "Trial Design" section under "METHODS", it states: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." In Figure 1 (CONSORT flow diagram) on page 3, one of the main arms is labeled "Placebo + docetaxel + ADT." In Figure 2 (OS in subgroups) on page 6, the legend for the control arm is "Placebo + ADT + docetaxel." In Figure 3 (Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer) on page 7, the legend for the control arm is "Placebo + ADT + docetaxel."

The median age for the treatment arm (Darolutamide) is reported in Table 1, "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS," on page 5. Locate the row "Age, median, years (range)" and the column "All Patients (N = 1,305) Darolutamide (n = 652)". The value is 67.0.

The median age for the control arm (Placebo) is reported in Table 1, "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS," on page 5. Locate the row "Age, median, years (range)" and the column "All Patients (N = 1,305) Placebo (n = 650)". The value is 67.0.

On page 3, refer to the CONSORT flow diagram (Figure 1). Locate the box for "Placebo + docetaxel + ADT" and find the number of patients "Analyzed for primary end point", which is n = 654. Alternatively, on page 5, refer to Table 1, "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS". In the "All Patients (N = 1,305)" column, under the "Placebo" sub-column, the total number of patients is 654.

The median follow-up duration for the entire study population is not explicitly reported in the PDF. While median treatment durations are mentioned (e.g., 41 months for darolutamide group, 34.1 months for placebo group on page 11, text), this is distinct from the overall median follow-up duration for the trial participants.

The abstract on page 1 (Chunk 8) states, "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." The Methods section on page 2 (Chunk 8) further clarifies, "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." The Discussion section on page 6 (Chunk 9) explicitly confirms, "The results of this post hoc analysis of the ARASENS trial, in which all patients received docetaxel, show that darolutamide in combination with ADT and docetaxel increases OS in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." Figure 1 (Chunk 0) on page 3 shows that 651 patients in the Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT arm received allocated treatment. Therefore, 651 patients (100%) in the treatment arm received docetaxel.

The abstract on page 1 (Chunk 8) states, "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." The Methods section on page 2 (Chunk 8) further clarifies, "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." The Discussion section on page 6 (Chunk 9) explicitly confirms, "The results of this post hoc analysis of the ARASENS trial, in which all patients received docetaxel, show that darolutamide in combination with ADT and docetaxel increases OS in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." Figure 1 (Chunk 0) on page 3 shows that 651 patients in the Placebo + docetaxel + ADT arm received allocated treatment. Therefore, 651 patients (100%) in the control arm received docetaxel.

The PubMed ID is not explicitly provided in the document. While there are DOIs in the references, a PubMed ID (PMID) is not listed.

The document is explicitly labeled "original reports" on the top left of page 1. The abstract and introduction describe it as an analysis of subgroups from the ARASENS trial, which is presented as a primary report of this specific subgroup analysis.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: From Table 3, under "All Patients" and "Darolutamide (n = 652)", sum the counts and percentages for "Grade 3", "Grade 4", and "Grade 5 (death)". Grade 3: 248 (38.0%), Grade 4: 183 (28.1%), Grade 5 (death): 27 (4.1%). Total: 248 + 183 + 27 = 458 patients. Total percentage: 38.0% + 28.1% + 4.1% = 70.2%. Value: 458 (70.2%)
columns.column_name_raw: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Control
columns.extraction_plan: From Table 3, under "All Patients" and "Placebo (n = 650)", sum the counts and percentages for "Grade 3", "Grade 4", and "Grade 5 (death)". Grade 3: 232 (35.7%), Grade 4: 181 (27.8%), Grade 5 (death): 26 (4.0%). Total: 232 + 181 + 26 = 439 patients. Total percentage: 35.7% + 27.8% + 4.0% = 67.5%. Value: 439 (67.5%)

Footnote 'd' for Table 3 states "Specific grade 5 events are provided in the supplementary appendix of Smith et al.", implying these are all-cause.

columns.extraction_plan: The trial design section on page 2 states, "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." This indicates two arms: one receiving darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel, and the other receiving placebo plus ADT and docetaxel. Figure 1 on page 3, the CONSORT flow diagram, also clearly shows two distinct arms: "Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT" and "Placebo + docetaxel + ADT".

The overall number and percentage of participants in the treatment arm with a Gleason score of ≤ 7 is not reported. Table 1 (page 5) provides the Gleason score distribution for the entire study population and for treatment/control arms within specific subgroups, but not for the overall treatment arm.

The overall number and percentage of participants in the control arm with a Gleason score of ≤ 7 is not reported. Table 1 (page 5) provides the Gleason score distribution for the entire study population and for treatment/control arms within specific subgroups, but not for the overall control arm.

The overall number and percentage of participants in the treatment arm with a Gleason score of ≥ 8 is not reported. Table 1 (page 5) provides the Gleason score distribution for the entire study population and for treatment/control arms within specific subgroups, but not for the overall treatment arm.

The value is reported in Figure 2A on page 6. Locate the Kaplan-Meier curve for "Placebo + ADT + docetaxel" under "High-volume disease" and extract the median OS. This is also stated in the "Primary End Point" section of the text on page 4.

The value is reported in Figure 2B on page 6. Locate the Kaplan-Meier curve for "Darolutamide + ADT + docetaxel" under "Low-volume disease" and extract the median OS. This is also stated in the "Primary End Point" section of the text on page 4.

The value is reported in Figure 2B on page 6. Locate the Kaplan-Meier curve for "Placebo + ADT + docetaxel" under "Low-volume disease" and extract the median OS. This is also stated in the "Primary End Point" section of the text on page 4.
The median on-treatment duration for the darolutamide treatment arm is reported in the text on page 11.

The median on-treatment duration for the overall control arm is not explicitly reported as a single value in this PDF. While median durations for the placebo group are provided for various subgroups (e.g., high-volume, low-volume, high-risk, low-risk) on page 4, an overall median for the entire control cohort is not stated.

Progression-Free Survival (PFS) rates for the overall population in the treatment arm are not reported in the PDF. The document reports "Time to CRPC" (Time to Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer) as a secondary endpoint, which is related but not the same as overall PFS.

Progression-Free Survival (PFS) rates for the overall population in the control arm are not reported in the PDF. The document reports "Time to CRPC" (Time to Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer) as a secondary endpoint, which is related but not the same as overall PFS.

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: Extract the count and percentage for "De novo mHSPC" from the "Darolutamide" arm by summing the values from the "High Volume" and "Low Volume" subgroups in Table 1, page 5. Darolutamide (High Volume): 432 (86.9%) Darolutamide (Low Volume): 126 (81.8%) Total Darolutamide patients (from Table 2 header): 651 Calculated total for "De novo mHSPC" in Darolutamide arm: 432 + 126 = 558 Calculated percentage: (558 / 651) * 100 = 85.7% Therefore, the value is 558 (85.7%).
columns.column_name_raw: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Treatment

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Control
columns.extraction_plan: Extract the count and percentage for "De novo mHSPC" from the "Placebo" arm by summing the values from the "High Volume" and "Low Volume" subgroups in Table 1, page 5. Placebo (High Volume): 445 (87.6%) Placebo (Low Volume): 121 (82.9%) Total Placebo patients (from Table 2 header): 654 Calculated total for "De novo mHSPC" in Placebo arm: 445 + 121 = 566 Calculated percentage: (566 / 654) * 100 = 86.5% Therefore, the value is 566 (86.5%).
columns.column_name_raw: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Control

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: Extract the count and percentage for "Recurrent mHSPC" from the "Darolutamide" arm by summing the values from the "High Volume" and "Low Volume" subgroups in Table 1, page 5. Darolutamide (High Volume): 58 (11.7%) Darolutamide (Low Volume): 28 (18.2%) Total Darolutamide patients (from Table 2 header): 651 Calculated total for "Recurrent mHSPC" in Darolutamide arm: 58 + 28 = 86 Calculated percentage: (86 / 651) * 100 = 13.2% Therefore, the value is 86 (13.2%).
columns.column_name_raw: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Treatment

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Control
columns.extraction_plan: Extract the count and percentage for "Recurrent mHSPC" from the "Placebo" arm by summing the values from the "High Volume" and "Low Volume" subgroups in Table 1, page 5. Placebo (High Volume): 59 (11.6%) Placebo (Low Volume): 23 (15.8%) Total Placebo patients (from Table 2 header): 654 Calculated total for "Recurrent mHSPC" in Placebo arm: 59 + 23 = 82 Calculated percentage: (82 / 654) * 100 = 12.5% Therefore, the value is 82 (12.5%).
columns.column_name_raw: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Control

columns.extraction_plan: The primary endpoint is explicitly stated in the "Study Assessments" subsection of the "METHODS" section. On page 3, in the first paragraph under "Study Assessments", the text reads: "OS was the primary end point."

Extract the number of deaths for the darolutamide group from the caption of Figure 1. The caption states: "The primary end point (OS) was assessed after 533 patients had died (229 in the darolutamide group and 304 in the placebo group)." The value for the darolutamide (treatment) group is 229.

Extract the number of deaths for the placebo group from the caption of Figure 1. The caption states: "The primary end point (OS) was assessed after 533 patients had died (229 in the darolutamide group and 304 in the placebo group)." The value for the placebo (control) group is 304.

The PDF reports on "time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire" (page 3, text, "Study Assessments" section, and page 8, Table 2). While this is a patient-reported outcome related to quality of life, it specifically measures physical symptoms rather than an overall "Quality of Life Scale" assessing general well-being and satisfaction with health status as per the definition. Therefore, an overall Quality of Life Scale is not reported.

The document reports "Time to CRPC" (Time to Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer) as a secondary efficacy endpoint, which is a measure of progression. For the "Overall" group in the Darolutamide arm, the median "Time to CRPC" is "NR" (Not Reached). This can be found on page 8, in Table 2, under the row "Time to CRPC" and the column "Darolutamide (n=651)".

The document reports "Time to CRPC" (Time to Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer) as a secondary efficacy endpoint, which is a measure of progression. For the "Overall" group in the Placebo arm, the median "Time to CRPC" is "19.1" months. This can be found on page 8, in Table 2, under the row "Time to CRPC" and the column "Placebo (n=654)".

This can be found on page 8, in Table 2, under the row "Time to CRPC" and the column "Darolutamide (n=497)".

Yes. The clinical trial reports results on quality of life. On page 3, in the "Study Assessments" section, it states: "Secondary efficacy end points included ... time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire". Table 2 on page 8 also lists "Time to worsening disease-related symptoms" as a secondary efficacy end point.

columns.extraction_plan: The secondary endpoints are explicitly listed in the "Study Assessments" section on page 3. They are: Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer, Time to pain progression on the basis of the Brief Pain Inventory Short-Form questionnaire, Symptomatic skeletal event-free survival, Time to first symptomatic-skeletal event, Time to initiation of subsequent systemic antineoplastic therapy, Time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire, Time to initiation of opioid use for 7 or more days. These endpoints are also presented as row headers in Table 2, "Secondary Efficacy End Points for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS", on page 8.

The total number of participants is 1,306. This value can be found in the "RESULTS" section on page 3, which states: "As previously reported, a total of 1,306 patients were randomly assigned...". This is also consistent with Figure 1 (CONSORT flow diagram) on page 3, which shows "Patients randomly assigned (N = 1,306)".
The PDF reports "M1c, visceral metastases ± LN or bone" in Table 1 (page 5), but does not provide a breakdown of specific visceral sites such as liver metastases for the treatment arm.

Locate Table 1: "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS" on page 5. Find the row "Metastasis stage at screening, No. (%)", and sub-row "M1b, bone metastases ± LN". Sum the counts for Darolutamide from "High Volume" (n=497) and "Low Volume" (n=154) subgroups: 386 + 131 = 517. The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 497 + 154 = 651. Calculate the percentage: (517 / 651) * 100 = 79.4%. Value: 517 (79.4%)

Locate Table 1: "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS" on page 5. Find the row "Metastasis stage at screening, No. (%)", and sub-row "M1b, bone metastases ± LN". Sum the counts for Placebo from "High Volume" (n=508) and "Low Volume" (n=146) subgroups: 390 + 131 = 521. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 508 + 146 = 654. Calculate the percentage: (521 / 654) * 100 = 79.7%. Value: 521 (79.7%)

Locate Table 1: "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS" on page 5. Find the row "Metastasis stage at screening, No. (%)", and sub-row "M1a, nonregional LN only". Sum the counts for Darolutamide from "High Volume" (n=497) and "Low Volume" (n=154) subgroups: 0 + 23 = 23. The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 497 + 154 = 651. Calculate the percentage: (23 / 651) * 100 = 3.5%. Value: 23 (3.5%)

Locate Table 1: "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS" on page 5. Find the row "Metastasis stage at screening, No. (%)", and sub-row "M1a, nonregional LN only". Sum the counts for Placebo from "High Volume" (n=508) and "Low Volume" (n=146) subgroups: 0 + 15 = 15. The total number of patients in the Placebo arm is 508 + 146 = 654. Calculate the percentage: (15 / 654) * 100 = 2.3%. Value: 15 (2.3%)

Extract the regimen for the experimental arm from the "PURPOSE" section of the abstract on page 1: "darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." The darolutamide arm is the experimental arm. Further confirmation is found in the "Trial Design" section on page 2: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel."

The experimental treatment arm is reported in the abstract and methods section. Extract the name of the arm from the "METHODS" section on page 1, which states: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." The experimental arm is the one receiving darolutamide.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The text on page 2, in the "METHODS" section, states: "In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." "De novo" refers to synchronous disease. Additionally, Table 1 on page 5, under "Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)", includes "De novo mHSPC" as a subgroup. Figure A1 on page 16 also presents OS results for the "De novo" subgroup under "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis".
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The text on page 2, in the "METHODS" section, states: "In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." "Recurrent" refers to metachronous disease. Additionally, Table 1 on page 5, under "Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)", includes "Recurrent mHSPC" as a subgroup. Figure A1 on page 16 also presents OS results for the "Recurrent" subgroup under "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis".
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The "PURPOSE" and "RESULTS" sections on page 1 explicitly mention reporting by disease volume. The "METHODS" section on page 2 defines "High-volume disease". Table 1 on page 5 is titled "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS" and includes a "High Volume" column. Figure 2A on page 6 shows OS for "high-volume disease". Table 2 on page 8, Table 3 on page 9, and Table 4 on page 10 all present data for the "High Volume" subgroup. Figure A1 on page 16 also presents OS results for the "High Volume" subgroup.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The "PURPOSE" and "RESULTS" sections on page 1 explicitly mention reporting by disease volume. The "METHODS" section on page 2 defines "Low-volume and low-risk disease". Table 1 on page 5 is titled "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS" and includes a "Low Volume" column. Figure 2B on page 6 shows OS for "low-volume disease". Table 2 on page 8, Table 3 on page 9, and Table 4 on page 10 all present data for the "Low Volume" subgroup. Figure A1 on page 17 also presents OS results for the "Low Volume" subgroup.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume

columns.extraction_plan: The trial name "ARASENS" is explicitly stated in the title of the paper and throughout the text.

Locate Table 2, titled "Secondary Efficacy End Points for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS". Find the row labeled "Time to CRPC". In the "All Patients" section, under the "Darolutamide (n = 651)" column, extract the median value. The value is "NR", which stands for "not reached" as per the table's abbreviations.

Locate Table 2, titled "Secondary Efficacy End Points for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS". Find the row labeled "Time to CRPC". In the "All Patients" section, under the "Placebo (n = 654)" column, extract the median value.

The type of therapy for the intervention arm is "Darolutamide plus androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) and docetaxel". This is explicitly stated in the title of the article and in the "METHODS" section of the abstract.

The number and percentage of participants with a Performance Status (PS) score of 0 in the Darolutamide treatment arm can be derived by summing the counts from the "High Volume" and "Low Volume" subgroups in Table 1. From Table 1, page 5, under "ECOG PS, No. (%)", for "0": Darolutamide (n = 497) High Volume: 343 (69.0%), Darolutamide (n = 154) Low Volume: 123 (79.9%). Summing these gives: 343 + 123 = 466 patients. The total number of Darolutamide patients in these subgroups is 497 + 154 = 651. The percentage is (466 / 651) * 100 = 71.6%. Therefore, the value is 466 (71.6%).

The number and percentage of participants with a Performance Status (PS) score of 0 in the Placebo control arm can be derived by summing the counts from the "High Volume" and "Low Volume" subgroups in Table 1. From Table 1, page 5, under "ECOG PS, No. (%)", for "0": Placebo (n = 508) High Volume: 349 (68.7%), Placebo (n = 146) Low Volume: 113 (77.4%). Summing these gives: 349 + 113 = 462 patients. The total number of Placebo patients in these subgroups is 508 + 146 = 654. The percentage is (462 / 654) * 100 = 70.6%. Therefore, the value is 462 (70.6%).

Table 1, page 5, reports ECOG PS scores as "0" or "1". No patients are reported with PS 2. Therefore, "PS 1-2" is interpreted as "PS 1". The number and percentage of participants with a Performance Status (PS) score of 1 in the Darolutamide treatment arm can be derived by summing the counts from the "High Volume" and "Low Volume" subgroups. From Table 1, page 5, under "ECOG PS, No. (%)", for "1": Darolutamide (n = 497) High Volume: 154 (31.0%), Darolutamide (n = 154) Low Volume: 31 (20.1%). Summing these gives: 154 + 31 = 185 patients. The total number of Darolutamide patients in these subgroups is 497 + 154 = 651. The percentage is (185 / 651) * 100 = 28.4%. Therefore, the value is 185 (28.4%).

Table 1, page 5, reports ECOG PS scores as "0" or "1". No patients are reported with PS 2. Therefore, "PS 1-2" is interpreted as "PS 1". The number and percentage of participants with a Performance Status (PS) score of 1 in the Placebo control arm can be derived by summing the counts from the "High Volume" and "Low Volume" subgroups. From Table 1, page 5, under "ECOG PS, No. (%)", for "1": Placebo (n = 508) High Volume: 157 (30.9%), Placebo (n = 146) Low Volume: 33 (22.6%). Summing these gives: 157 + 33 = 190 patients. The total number of Placebo patients in these subgroups is 508 + 146 = 654. The percentage is (190 / 654) * 100 = 29.0%. Therefore, the value is 190 (29.0%).

Extract the value from Table 1, under the "All Patients (N = 1,305)" column, row "Geographical region", sub-row "North America", for the "Darolutamide (n = 651)" arm. The count is 95. Calculate the percentage as (95 / 651) * 100% = 14.6%.

Extract the value from Table 1, under the "All Patients (N = 1,305)" column, row "Geographical region", sub-row "North America", for the "Placebo (n = 654)" arm. The count is 88. Calculate the percentage as (88 / 654) * 100% = 13.5%.

The treatment regimen consists of three classes of therapy. These are explicitly mentioned in the "INTRODUCTION" section on page 1 (Chunk 8) and the "abstract" on page 1 (Chunk 8). The combination of these three is also referred to as "triplet therapy" in the "Relevance" section on page 2 (Chunk 8).

For the Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT arm, extract the value '652' from the 'All Patients' column under 'Darolutamide' in Table 1. For the Placebo + docetaxel + ADT arm, extract the value '650' from the 'All Patients' column under 'Placebo' in Table 1.

The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 651 (from Figure 1, page 3, "Analyzed for primary end point"). Calculate the percentage as (497 / 651) * 100.

columns.extraction_plan: Page: 3. Source: Figure 1, text below the diagram. "The primary end point (OS) was assessed after 533 patients had died (229 in the darolutamide group and 304 in the placebo group)." The total number of patients analyzed for the primary endpoint in the darolutamide group was 651 (from Figure 1, "Analyzed for primary end point"). Value: Calculate as (Total patients - Number of deaths) / Total patients * 100 = (651 - 229) / 651 * 100 = 64.82%.

Page: 3. Source: Figure 1, text below the diagram. "The primary end point (OS) was assessed after 533 patients had died (229 in the darolutamide group and 304 in the placebo group)." The total number of patients analyzed for the primary endpoint in the placebo group was 654 (from Figure 1, "Analyzed for primary end point"). Value: Calculate as (Total patients - Number of deaths) / Total patients * 100 = (654 - 304) / 654 * 100 = 53.52%.

Page: 16. Source: Figure A1, Panel A, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - De novo", Darolutamide column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 181/432). This represents the high-volume synchronous subgroup. Page: 17. Source: Figure A1, Panel B, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - De novo", Darolutamide column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 25/126). This represents the low-volume synchronous subgroup. Value: Combine data from both subgroups. Total patients = 432 + 126 = 558. Total events = 181 + 25 = 206. Calculate as (Total patients - Total events) / Total patients * 100 = (558 - 206) / 558 * 100 = 63.08%.

Page: 16. Source: Figure A1, Panel A, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - De novo", Placebo column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 239/445). This represents the high-volume synchronous subgroup. Page: 17. Source: Figure A1, Panel B, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - De novo", Placebo column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 32/121). This represents the low-volume synchronous subgroup. Value: Combine data from both subgroups. Total patients = 445 + 121 = 566. Total events = 239 + 32 = 271. Calculate as (Total patients - Total events) / Total patients * 100 = (566 - 271) / 566 * 100 = 52.12%.

Page: 16. Source: Figure A1, Panel A, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - Recurrent", Darolutamide column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 21/58). This represents the high-volume metachronous subgroup. Page: 17. Source: Figure A1, Panel B, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - Recurrent", Darolutamide column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 1/28). This represents the low-volume metachronous subgroup. Value: Combine data from both subgroups. Total patients = 58 + 28 = 86. Total events = 21 + 1 = 22. Calculate as (Total patients - Total events) / Total patients * 100 = (86 - 22) / 86 * 100 = 74.42%.

Page: 16. Source: Figure A1, Panel A, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - Recurrent", Placebo column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 26/59). This represents the high-volume metachronous subgroup. Page: 17. Source: Figure A1, Panel B, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - Recurrent", Placebo column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 4/23). This represents the low-volume metachronous subgroup. Value: Combine data from both subgroups. Total patients = 59 + 23 = 82. Total events = 26 + 4 = 30. Calculate as (Total patients - Total events) / Total patients * 100 = (82 - 30) / 82 * 100 = 63.41%.

Page: 16. Source: Figure A1, Panel A, row "Overall", Darolutamide column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 203/497). As per the caption, Panel A is for high-volume disease, so this "Overall" row refers to the high-volume subgroup. Value: Calculate as (Total patients - Number of events) / Total patients * 100 = (497 - 203) / 497 * 100 = 59.10%.

Page: 16. Source: Figure A1, Panel A, row "Overall", Placebo column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 245/503). As per the caption, Panel A is for high-volume disease, so this "Overall" row refers to the high-volume subgroup. Value: Calculate as (Total patients - Number of events) / Total patients * 100 = (503 - 245) / 503 * 100 = 51.30%.

Page: 17. Source: Figure A1, Panel B, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - De novo", Darolutamide column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 25/126). This represents the low-volume synchronous subgroup. Page: 17. Source: Figure A1, Panel B, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - Recurrent", Darolutamide column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 1/28). This represents the low-volume metachronous subgroup. Value: Combine data from both subgroups. Total patients = 126 + 28 = 154. Total events = 25 + 1 = 26. Calculate as (Total patients - Total events) / Total patients * 100 = (154 - 26) / 154 * 100 = 83.12%.

Page: 17. Source: Figure A1, Panel B, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - De novo", Placebo column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 32/121). This represents the low-volume synchronous subgroup. Page: 17. Source: Figure A1, Panel B, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - Recurrent", Placebo column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 4/23). This represents the low-volume metachronous subgroup. Value: Combine data from both subgroups. Total patients = 121 + 23 = 144. Total events = 32 + 4 = 36. Calculate as (Total patients - Total events) / Total patients * 100 = (144 - 36) / 144 * 100 = 75.00%.

The table does not explicitly report an "Unknown" race category. While there are patients whose race is not accounted for by the sum of the explicit categories (651 total - 497 reported = 154 unaccounted), this is not labeled as "Unknown" in the table. The "not reported" part of "Other or not reported" might include some, but it's not a distinct "Unknown" category.
